schemeSubCategory.value: Education and training grants, fellowship, stipend
schemeSubCategory.label: Education and training grants, fellowship, stipend

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The Physical Research Laboratory (PRL), a prestigious institution under the Department of Space, Government of India, launched the Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) Program. The JRF program aims to provide highly motivated and academically excellent candidates with an opportunity to pursue cutting-edge research in a variety of science domains, such as Astronomy and astrophysics, atomic, Molecular, and optical Physics, Geosciences, Planetary Sciences, Space and Atmospheric Sciences, Solar Physics, and Theoretical Physics.
schemeContent.benefits_md: > **Fellowship Allowance:** • Junior Research Fellow (JRF): ₹37,000/- per month. • Senior Research Fellow (SRF, after 2 years): ₹42,000/- per month (based on performance review). > **Platinum Jubilee Research Fellowship (ATAL):** • Selected meritorious JRFs may receive an additional ATAL fellowship of ₹40,000/- per month (for 3rd to 5th year). > **Ph.D. Registration:** • Fellows have the opportunity to register for a Ph.D. at an institute/university with an MoU with PRL.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The Physical Research Laboratory (PRL), a prestigious institution under the Department of Space, Government of India, launched the Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) Program.

children.text: The Physical Research Laboratory (PRL), a prestigious institution under the Department of Space, Government of India, launched the Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) Program. The JRF program aims to provide highly motivated and academically excellent candidates with an opportunity to pursue cutting-edge research in a variety of science domains, such as Astronomy and astrophysics, atomic, Molecular, and optical Physics, Geosciences, Planetary Sciences, Space and Atmospheric Sciences, Solar Physics, and Theoretical Physics.
Fellowship Allowance:
•	Junior Research Fellow (JRF): ₹37,000/- per month.
•	Senior Research Fellow (SRF, after 2 years): ₹42,000/- per month (based on performance review).
Platinum Jubilee Research Fellowship (ATAL):
•	Selected meritorious JRFs may receive an additional ATAL fellowship of ₹40,000/- per month (for 3rd to 5th year).
Ph.D. Registration:
•	Fellows have the opportunity to register for a Ph.D. at an institute/university with an MoU with PRL.
Step 1:
The applicant visits the
PRL website
Step 2:
Click on "Register" and then fill in Personal Details, Qualification Details, and Experience Details.
Step 3:
Now select the Preferred Interview dates and then upload a passport-sized image. and other mandatory documents.
Step 4:
Submit the application by clicking on the "Submit" button.
User Manual:
Click here

applicationProcess.process_md: **Step 1:** The applicant visits the [PRL website](https://www.prl.res.in/jrfrecruit/). **Step 2:** Click on "Register" and then fill in Personal Details, Qualification Details, and Experience Details. **Step 3:** Now select the Preferred Interview dates and then upload a passport-sized image. and other mandatory documents. **Step 4:** Submit the application by clicking on the "Submit" button. > **User Manual:** [Click here](https://www.prl.res.in/jrfrecruit/howtoapply).

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
1. The applicant should be an Indian citizen.
1. The applicant should have studied at recognized Universities/Institutes in India.
1. The applicant’s age should be 28 years as of 01st July 2024.
1. The applicant should complete a Bachelor’s and Master’s degree in science or engineering with at least 60% marks (or equivalent) at both levels.
1. Candidates from disciplines of any branch of Physics, Chemistry, Engineering Physics, Photonics, Space Physics, Atmospheric Science, Environmental Science, Oceanography, Meteorology, Geology, Geophysics, Earth Science and Remote Sensing are eligible to apply.
1. The applicant should be interested in pursuing a research career in the current research areas at PRL, and must have qualified in any one of the following exams:



  - CSIR-UGC-NET JRF/AP (2022 or later) in Physical Sciences/Chemical Sciences/Earth, Atmospheric, Ocean, and Planetary Sciences.
  - GATE (2022 or later) in relevant subjects.
  - JEST (2024) in Physics.
  - UGC-NET JRF/AP (2022 or later) in Environmental Sciences.

children.text: The applicant should be an Indian citizen.
The applicant should have studied at recognized Universities/Institutes in India.
The applicant’s age should be 28 years as of 01st July 2024.
The applicant should complete a Bachelor’s and Master’s degree in science or engineering with at least 60% marks (or equivalent) at both levels.

Candidates from disciplines of any branch of Physics, Chemistry, Engineering Physics, Photonics, Space Physics, Atmospheric Science, Environmental Science, Oceanography, Meteorology, Geology, Geophysics, Earth Science and Remote Sensing are eligible to apply.
The applicant should be interested in pursuing a research career in the current research areas at PRL, and must have qualified in any one of the following exams:
CSIR-UGC-NET JRF/AP (2022 or later) in Physical Sciences/Chemical Sciences/Earth, Atmospheric, Ocean, and Planetary Sciences.
GATE (2022 or later) in relevant subjects.
JEST (2024) in Physics.
UGC-NET JRF/AP (2022 or later) in Environmental Sciences.
